title: Ceropegia omissa
taxonomy: Ceropegia omissa is classified in the family Apocynaceae. This family comprises a large number of plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. Ceropegia omissa is a species of flowering plant within the genus Ceropegia. The genus Ceropegia is further grouped under the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. The subfamily Asclepiadoideae includes various species of milkweed and related plants.

intro: Ceropegia omissa, commonly known as the "milk-pair vine" or "vine with inverted flowers," is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Ceropegia omissa is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Ceropegia omissa is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is commonly used to treat skin conditions, respiratory issues, and fever, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
